root_text.an10.48:4.1: ‘Añño me ākappo karaṇīyo’ti pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.48:5.1: ‘Kacci nu kho me attā sīlato na upavadatī’ti pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.48:6.1: ‘Kacci nu kho maṁ anuvicca viññū sabrahmacārī sīlato na upavadantī’ti pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.48:7.1: ‘Sabbehi me piyehi manāpehi nānābhāvo vinābhāvo’ti pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.48:9.1: yaṁ kammaṁ karissāmi kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā tassa dāyādo bhavissāmī’ti pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.48:10.1: ‘Kathaṁbhūtassa me rattindivā vītivattantī’ti pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.48:11.1: ‘Kacci nu kho ahaṁ suññāgāre abhiramāmī’ti pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.48:12.1: ‘Atthi nu kho me uttari manussadhammo alamariyañāṇadassanaviseso adhigato, yenāhaṁ pacchime kāle sabrahmacārīhi puṭṭho na maṅku bhavissāmī’ti pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.48:13.1: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dasa dhammā pabbajitena abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbā”ti.

translation_text.an10.48:0.3: Ten Regular Reflections for a Renunciate
translation_text.an10.48:1.1: “Mendicants, one who has gone forth should often review these ten things.
translation_text.an10.48:2.1: One who has gone forth should often review this: ‘I have secured freedom from class.’
translation_text.an10.48:3.1: ‘My livelihood is tied up with others.’
translation_text.an10.48:5.1: ‘I hope there’s no reason to blame myself when it comes to ethical conduct?’
translation_text.an10.48:6.1: ‘I hope that, after examination, sensible spiritual companions don’t reproach any aspect of my ethics?’
translation_text.an10.48:7.1: ‘I must be parted and separated from all I hold dear and beloved.’
translation_text.an10.48:8.1: ‘I am the owner of my deeds and heir to my deeds. Deeds are my womb, my relative, and my refuge.
translation_text.an10.48:9.1: I shall be the heir of whatever deeds I do, whether good or bad.’
translation_text.an10.48:10.1: ‘As the days and nights pass by, what sort of person am I becoming?’
translation_text.an10.48:11.1: ‘Do I love to stay in empty huts?’
translation_text.an10.48:12.1: ‘Do I have any superhuman distinctions in knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones, so that when my spiritual companions question me on my deathbed I will not be embarrassed?’
translation_text.an10.48:13.1: One who has gone forth should often review these ten things.”
